TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify fresh agent installation creates vault database files
Steps:
1. Perform fresh agent installation using dcregister binary
2. Navigate to agent install directory /data/datavault/
3. Verify agent.db exists under /data/datavault/storage/
4. Verify m_vault.db exists under /data/datavault/vault/
5. Verify both files have 0600 permissions (owner read/write only)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify agent install time is stored in vault on fresh install
Steps:
1. Perform fresh agent installation
2. After registration completes check agent logs for 'StoreAgentInstallTime' entry
3. Run: dctroubleshootingtool --db /usr/local/manageengine/uems_agent/data/datavault/storage/agent.db "SELECT * FROM auth WHERE key='agent_install_time'"
4. Verify the stored value is a valid epoch timestamp close to current time

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify auth tokens are stored in vault DB during registration
Steps:
1. Perform fresh agent registration (OP or Cloud)
2. Verify no auth-props.json or approval-props.json files are created under /data/
3. Check dcregister logs for 'Stored approval_token' and 'Stored auth_token' entries
4. Verify /data/datavault/vault/m_vault.db is non-empty (encrypted data present)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify CSR private key and client cert stored in vault not files
Steps:
1. Perform fresh agent registration with server that issues CSR
2. Verify /data/certificates/certpk.crt file is NOT created
3. Verify /data/certificates/clientcert.crt file is NOT created
4. Check dcregister logs for 'csr_private_key' and 'csr_client_cert' vault store entries

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify proxy password stored in vault DB during registration
Steps:
1. Configure agent with proxy server (proxy with authentication)
2. Perform fresh agent registration through proxy
3. Verify proxy_password is not stored in server-info.json (M_proxyPassword should be empty or absent)
4. Verify agent connects through proxy successfully after registration (proves password loaded from vault)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify provisioning key stored in vault on cloud registration
Steps:
1. Perform cloud agent registration
2. Check dcregister logs for 'prov_key' vault store entry
3. Verify server-info.json does not contain decrypted provisioning key
4. Verify agent can communicate with server post-registration

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify vault DB files not world-readable
Steps:
1. Perform fresh agent installation
2. Run: ls -la /data/datavault/storage/ and /data/datavault/vault/
3. Verify all .db files have permissions -rw------- (0600)
4. Verify datavault directories have permissions drwx------ (0700)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade from pre-vault version migrates auth data to vault DB
Steps:
1. Install agent with version < 11.5.2620.01.L (e.g. 11.5.2606.01.L)
2. Verify auth-props.json and approval-props.json exist under /data/
3. Trigger agent upgrade to latest version (via server push or manual)
4. Wait for dcupgrader to complete
5. Verify vault DB files created under /data/datavault/
6. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated approval_token' and 'Migrated auth_token' entries
7. Verify agent communicates with server successfully after upgrade (proves migrated tokens work)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates approval_token from approval-props.json
Steps:
1. Install old agent version with approval-props.json containing M_value1 and M_value2
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated approval_token to vault DB'
4. After upgrade verify agent WFA (Waiting For Approval) flow works correctly

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates auth_token from auth-props.json
Steps:
1. Install old agent version with auth-props.json containing M_value1 and M_value2
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated auth_token to vault DB'
4. After upgrade verify agent server communication works (auth token used in HTTP headers)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates provisioning key from server-info
Steps:
1. Install old cloud agent with prov_key in server-info.json (M_value1/M_value2)
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated prov_key to vault DB'
4. Verify agent re-registration or server communication succeeds post-upgrade

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates proxy_password from server-info
Steps:
1. Install old agent with proxy configured (M_proxyPassword in server-info.json)
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated proxy_password to vault DB'
4. Verify agent can communicate through proxy after upgrade

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates CSR private key from certpk.crt
Steps:
1. Install old agent that has /data/certificates/certpk.crt
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated csr_private_key to vault DB'
4. Verify HTTPS client cert authentication works after upgrade

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates client certificate from clientcert.crt
Steps:
1. Install old agent that has /data/certificates/clientcert.crt
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated csr_client_cert to vault DB'
4. Verify TLS mutual auth works after upgrade

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates ds_auth_token when DS auth is enabled
Steps:
1. Install old agent with dsAuthEnabled=true in approval-props.json and M_value10 set
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated ds_auth_token to vault DB from approval-props'
4. Verify DS communication works after upgrade

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates agentInstallTime from SystemDetails.json
Steps:
1. Install old agent with AgentInstallTime set in SystemDetails.json
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Migrated agentInstallTime to plain vault DB'
4. Verify vault encryption works post-upgrade (install time used as salt)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade from already-vault version skips migration
Steps:
1. Install agent with version >= 11.5.2614.03.L
2. Trigger upgrade to newer version
3. Check dcupgrader logs for 'Skipping auth data migration to vault DB'
4. Verify existing vault data is untouched

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade handles missing approval-props.json gracefully
Steps:
1. Install old agent without approval-props.json (agent not yet approved)
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Verify dcupgrader does not crash or panic
4. Verify auth-props.json is used as fallback source
5. Check logs for appropriate messages

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade handles missing auth-props.json gracefully
Steps:
1. Install old agent without auth-props.json
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Verify no crash or panic in dcupgrader
4. Verify other migration steps (prov_key/proxy/certs) still complete

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify upgrade migrates dynamic_key and approval_key from file value2 fields
Steps:
1. Install old agent with auth-props.json (M_value2) and approval-props.json (M_value2)
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Check dcupgrader logs for dynamic_key and approval_key storage
4. Verify post-upgrade communication uses decrypted dynamic key from vault

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify binary checksum cleared on installer checksum mismatch
Steps:
1. Tamper with the downloaded installer binary checksum
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Verify dcupgrader logs show checksum mismatch error
4. Verify binary_checksum entry is cleared from vault
5. Verify error status posted to server
6. On next upgrade attempt verify fresh binary checksum is downloaded

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify old renamed binary files are removed during upgrade from < 2606.01
Steps:
1. Install agent version < 11.5.2606.01.L with old binary names (dcrdsagent etc.)
2. Trigger upgrade
3. Verify StartSelfServicePortal.sh removed
4. Verify dcrdsagent removed
5. Verify dctoolsNotification removed
6. Verify SelfServicePortal removed

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify dcconfig downloads and processes service-master-keys metadata
Steps:
1. Configure server with service-mkeys.json under /client-data/ with SOM and FRAMEWORK UUIDs
2. Add customer-meta-data.json entry with meta_data_key=service-master-keys
3. Wait for dcconfig refresh cycle or trigger manual refresh
4. Check dcconfig logs for 'ProcessServiceMasterKeys: starting'
5. Verify keyShareRequest is sent to server (check server access logs)
6. Check dcconfig logs for 'stored key for keyUUID' entries

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify service-master-keys retry on decryption failure
Steps:
1. Set up service-mkeys.json with valid UUIDs on server
2. Temporarily make the dynamic key invalid (or corrupt vault)
3. Trigger dcconfig refresh
4. Verify logs show 'unable to decrypt keyShareRequest response'
5. Verify metadata version is NOT updated for service-master-keys
6. Restore correct dynamic key
7. Trigger next refresh cycle
8. Verify keys are fetched and stored successfully on retry

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify only applicable module keys are stored (SOM and FRAMEWORK)
Steps:
1. Configure server service-mkeys.json with UUIDs for SOM FRAMEWORK PATCH EDR
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh
3. Verify logs show 'skipping non-applicable module: PATCH' and 'skipping non-applicable module: EDR'
4. Verify only SOM and FRAMEWORK keys are stored in vault
5. Verify SSMK_ID_SOM and SSMK_ID_FRAMEWORK mappings created

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Invalid module entry in keyShareRequest response is skipped
Steps:
1. Configure server to return a response containing an entry with moduleName=Invalid
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh
3. Verify logs show 'no actionable data for keyUUID' for the Invalid entry
4. Verify processing continues for other valid entries
5. Verify overall processing succeeds

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify NotApplicable module entry in keyShareRequest response is skipped
Steps:
1. Configure server to return a response with moduleName=NotApplicable
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh
3. Verify logs show 'skipping non-applicable module: NotApplicable'
4. Verify the NotApplicable keyUUID is not stored in vault
5. Verify other applicable keys are processed normally

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify isKeyAtAdaptionStage=true uses static master key as service key
Steps:
1. Configure server keyShareRequest response with isKeyAtAdaptionStage=true for FRAMEWORK
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh
3. Verify logs show 'key at adaption stage for keyUUID'
4. Verify FRAMEWORK key stored successfully in vault
5. Verify the stored key value is the static master key (validate by comparing encrypted operations)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify timestamp updated only on successful key processing
Steps:
1. Configure server with 3 keyUUIDs: SOM (valid) FRAMEWORK (invalid serviceKey) SOM2 (valid)
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh
3. Verify SOM timestamp updated in smkeys_last_contact table
4. Verify FRAMEWORK timestamp NOT updated (decryption failed)
5. Verify overall ProcessServiceMasterKeys returns false (partial failure)
6. Verify metadata version NOT updated

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify first-time service-master-keys requests all keyUUIDs
Steps:
1. Fresh agent with no prior service-master-keys processing
2. Set up service-mkeys.json with 3 keyUUIDs
3. Trigger dcconfig refresh
4. Verify keyShareRequest contains all 3 keyUUIDs in the POST body
5. Verify logs show 'first run — requesting all keyUUIDs'

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify changed createdTime triggers re-request for that keyUUID only
Steps:
1. Process service-master-keys successfully (all timestamps stored)
2. Update service-mkeys.json on server with changed createdTime for one keyUUID
3. Trigger dcconfig refresh
4. Verify keyShareRequest only contains the changed keyUUID
5. Verify logs show 'createdTime changed for keyUUID'

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify unchanged service-mkeys does not trigger keyShareRequest
Steps:
1. Process service-master-keys successfully
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh again without any server-side changes
3. Verify logs show 'no keys need requesting. All up to date'
4. Verify no keyShareRequest sent to server

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify new applicable module addition triggers request for unresolved keys
Steps:
1. Successfully process service-master-keys with SOM and FRAMEWORK
2. Add a new module (e.g. PATCH) to applicableModules in a future version
3. Update service-mkeys.json with a new keyUUID for PATCH
4. Trigger dcconfig refresh
5. Verify the new PATCH keyUUID is requested
6. Verify existing unchanged keys are not re-requested

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify agent-server communication uses auth token from vault
Steps:
1. Complete agent registration
2. Verify auth token is in vault DB (check logs)
3. Trigger any server communication (e.g. dcconfig refresh)
4. Verify HTTP request includes auth token in headers
5. Verify server responds successfully (token accepted)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Waiting For Approval flow works with vault-based tokens
Steps:
1. Register agent with WFA enabled on server
2. Verify approval_token stored in vault (check dcregister logs)
3. Verify agent polls for approval status
4. Approve agent from server console
5. Verify agent receives auth_token and dynamic_key
6. Verify both stored in vault (check logs)
7. Verify agent communicates normally after approval

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify auth token refresh stores new token in vault
Steps:
1. Agent running with valid auth token in vault
2. Trigger auth token refresh from server (AuthProps GET)
3. Verify new auth_token and dynamic_key stored in vault
4. Verify old tokens replaced
5. Verify agent continues communication with new token

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify DS auth token stored in vault when dsAuth is enabled

2. Register agent
3. Verify ds_auth_enabled stored as 'true' in vault
4. Verify ds_auth_token stored in encrypted vault

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify agent uninstall completes without crash or SIGSEGV
Steps:

2. Trigger agent uninstall via dcregister -u or server push
3. Verify uninstall process completes without segmentation fault
4. Verify no fatal error in uninstall logs

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify vault DB files removed on clean uninstall
Steps:

2. Trigger clean uninstall
3. Verify /data/datavault/ directory and all contents removed
4. Verify no residual .db files left behind

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify agent restarts and loads all tokens from vault correctly
Steps:

2. Stop agent service (dcservice -p)
3. Start agent service
4. Verify agent communicates with server successfully on restart
5. Verify no token-related errors in dcconfig or dcservice logs
6. Verify no file-based auth fallback (no auth-props.json read)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify long-running agent process does not lose vault access

2. Let agent run for extended period (24+ hours)
3. Trigger dcconfig refresh multiple times
4. Verify each refresh successfully reads tokens from vault
5. Verify no memory-related crashes (no SIGSEGV in logs)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify simultaneous dcconfig and dcpatchscan do not corrupt vault
Steps:
1. Install and register agent
2. Trigger dcconfig refresh and dcpatchscan simultaneously
3. Verify both processes complete without error
4. Verify vault DB not corrupted (agent continues to function)
5. Verify no deadlock (both processes complete within timeout)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify multiple vault readers do not cause deadlock
Steps:
1. Agent running with multiple scheduled tasks (dcconfig dcinventory dcpatchscan)
2. Trigger all tasks near-simultaneously
3. Verify all tasks complete without deadlock
4. Verify agent logs show no mutex-related timeout or error

5. Verify build completes for both AMD64 and ARM64 architectures

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify meaap binary builds with GOPATH fallback
